rulings.Case Title: REA RENE LAMPARAS-MCCULLOGH VS. PENSON INC (DBA PENSON TRUST COMPANY) et al
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON WEDNESDAY, DECEMBER 5, 2007, LINE 6, DEFENDANT PENSCO, INC. (DBA PENSCO TRUST COMPANY),, JAMES GUCKIAN'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT. SUSTAINED IN PART, OVERRULED IN PART, 10 DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND. 2ND CAUSE OF ACTION SUSTAINED. PLAINTIFF HAS NOT ALLEGED RETALIATORY CONDUCT BY GUCKIAN; 5TH CAUSE OF ACTION SUSTAINED. AN EMPLYEE MAY BE LIABLE FOR RETALIATION BUT PLAINTIFF HAS NOT ALLEGED RETALIATORY CONDUCT BY GUCKIAN. 6TH AND 7TH CAUSES OF ACTION SUSTAINED. NEEDS TO PLEAD FACTS SUPPORTING THE CONTENTION THAT THE ACTS WERE WITHIN THE COURSE AND SCOPE OF EMPLOYMENT. 8TH AND 9TH CAUSES OF ACTION OVERRULED AS TO PENSCO, INC., SUSTAINED AS TO 9TH CAUSE OF ACTION AS TO GUCKIAN BECAUSE PLAINTIFF HAS NOT ALLEGED NEGLIGENT ACTS OR OMISSIONS BY GUCKIAN. =(302/PJM/VC)

rulings.Case Title: REA RENE LAMPARAS-MCCULLOGH VS. PENSON INC (DBA PENSON TRUST COMPANY) et al
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON WEDNESDAY, DECEMBER 5, 2007, LINE 6, DEFENDANT PENSCO, INC. (DBA PENSCO TRUST COMPANY),, JAMES GUCKIAN'S MOTION TO STRIKE 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT. GRANTED AS TO 1ST CAUSE OF ACTION, 10 DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND. THE 1ST AND 5TH CAUSES OF ACTION APPEAR REDUNDANT FOR THE CLAIMS APPEAR ONLY TO BE BASED ON FEHA. IF PLAINTIFF RELIES ON DIFFERENT FEHA SECTIONS, PLAINTIFF SHOULD SO STATE. GRANTED AS TO PARAGRAPHS 9,11 AND PAGE 3, LINES 17-19. PLAINTIFF NEEDS TO ALLEGE FACTS TO SUPPORT A CONTINUING VIOLATIONS CLAIM. =(302/PJM/VC)

rulings.Case Title: COLLEEN BARGER-CAREY VS. REPUBLIC INDEMNITY COMPANY OF AMERICA et al
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON WEDNESDAY, DECEMBER 5, 2007, LINE 7, DEFENDANT REPUBLIC INDEMNITY COMPANY OF AMERICA'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT. OVERRULED AS TO 1ST AND 2ND CAUSES OF ACTION. THE COMPLAINT ALLEGES A BERACH OF THE OBLIGATION TO PAY A WORKER'S COMPENSATION CLAIM AND A BREACH OF THE DUTY TO DEFEND. A DEMURRER MAY ONLY CHALLENGE THE COMPLAINT AND NO SHOWING HAS BEEN MADE THAT COLLATERAL ESTOPPEL WOULD APPLY. SUSTAINED AS TO 3RD AND 4TH CAUSES OF ACTION; 10 DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND TO PLEAD FACTS TO SUPPORT THE ALLEGED LEGAL CONCLUSIONS. =(302/PJM/AY)

rulings.Case Title: TRUST OF REGAN CARROLL et al VS. CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Hearing On Petition For Writ Of Mandate; Exhibits 1-15, 16-27,28-31.
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON WEDNESDAY, DECEMBER 5, 2007, LINE 14, PLAINTIFF TRUST OF REGAN CARROLL'S Notice Of Hearing On Petition For Writ Of Mandate; Exhibits 1-15, 16-27,28-31. The writ is denied. Government Code section 65942 provides that a permit application is not complete where there has been a subsequent enactment of new local requirements by a local agency. The Board of Supervisors acted as a local agency in designating the Dogpatch neighborhood as a historic neighborhood. This is a substantial change and that designation requires that Petitioner obtain a Certificate of Appropriateness. The fact that the negative declaration noted the possibility of this occurring does not mean that this application was considered based on the criteria applicable to a historic neighborhood. There is no evidence that it was so considered. The issuance of a permit without compliance with the Certificate of Appropriateness would be contrary to the City's current land use law. Land Waste Management v. Contra Costa County Board of Supervisors (1990) 222 Cal.App.3d 950, 959. It also appears that Respondent's objection to the Carroll Declaration is well taken for the material does not appear relevant to the legal issue raised by this writ. =(302/PJM)
